mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year, real rates turn negative, eroding cash returns and forcing equity risk premium compression. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedge over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-05-01, FEDFUNDS=0.06% and CPIAUCSL=268.551 (May 2021 CPI at 4.7% YoY)
regime_hint: post-pandemic monetary accommodation with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window during the trigger regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.0% signals tightening labor markets and wage pressure, which equity investors interpret as earnings growth headwind. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.8% as of 2021-05-01, already below 6.0% threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE and 5-day forward returns is positive (opposite of predicted direction) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) growing faster than 3% quarter-over-quarter signals robust consumer demand and earnings growth. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices corporate profit expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=620214M as of 2021-05-01, showing post-pandemic recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC between real sales growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in more than 50% of periods with real sales growth > 3% QoQ.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average, equity investors reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=99.8562 as of 2021-05-01, near pre-pandemic levels but showing volatility
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns is positive (opposite of predicted direction) over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1.5 million annualized units signals strong residential investment demand and forward economic momentum. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained demand-driven growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.5 million annualized units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1569.0 thousand units as of 2021-04-01, above 1.5M threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST and 60-day forward returns is negative over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerating above 300k jobs per month while UNRATE remains above 5.5% signals labor market slack and Fed accommodation persistence. This interaction mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as accommodative policy supports equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=144894k as of 2021-05-01 with strong post-pandemic recovery, UNRATE=5.8%
regime_hint: post-pandemic recovery with persistent slack and policy support
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains above 1.5%, the Fed faces a growth-inflation dilemma. Equity investors interpret this as policy error risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as volatility spikes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=19088.064B (2021-Q1 vintage) showing post-pandemic recovery, FEDFUNDS=0.06% as of 2021-05-01
regime_hint: policy constraint regime with slowing growth and elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.4 million annualized units signals synchronized demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly change < 0% AND HOUST < 1.4 million annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=620214M and HOUST=1569k as of 2021-04-01, both showing strength but vulnerable to reversal
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.025 over any rolling 18-month window during demand-weak regimes.
